title: Mildly Seasoned Uruguayan Beef Empanadas
description: A traditional Uruguayan dish featuring seasoned beef and fermented dough, perfect for a low-carb meal prep option. These empanadas are pillowy and delicious, with a hint of rice vinegar flavor.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 375 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a large bowl, combine the ground beef, chopped onion, and minced garlic, and mix well.
Add the seasoned rice vinegar to the beef mixture and stir to combine.
In a separate bowl, whisk together the cauliflower flour and eggs to form a dough.
Knead the dough for 5 minutes until it becomes pliable and smooth.
Divide the dough into 8 equal pieces and roll each piece into a ball.
Flatten each ball into a disk shape and place a tablespoon of the beef mixture in the center of each disk.
Fold the dough over the filling to form a half-moon shape and press the edges together to seal.
Brush the tops of the empanadas with a little water and sprinkle with sodium alginate.
Bake the empanadas for 20 minutes, or until golden brown.
Cook ground beef to an internal temperature of 71°C / 160°F.
